occurrence_sentence: Google's CEO Sundar Pichai sounded cautiously optimistic in comments about the paper that describes how scientists using Google's 53-bit quantum computer, named Sycamore, took 200 seconds to perform a calculation that would have taken the world's fastest supercomputer 10,000 years.

Google's CEO Sundar Pichai sounded cautiously optimistic in comments about the paper that describes how scientists using Google's 53-bit quantum computer, named Sycamore, took 200 seconds to perform a calculation that would have taken the world's fastest supercomputer 10,000 years.

In an interview with MIT's Technology Review, Pichai likened the performance of Sycamore to the Wright Brothers' first flight and expects more time and better technology to create a quantum computer that can achieve quantum supremacy in general fields, not just at a narrow task.
